channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: World Cup winners Ruiz, Gavi honoured with 150kg of tomatoes
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Spanish World Cup winners Fabián Ruiz and Gavi received 150kg of tomatoes from their hometown, Los Palacios y Villafranca, in a unique celebration.
Read More: https://punchng.com/world-cup-winners-ruiz-gavi-honoured-with-150kg-of-tomatoes/

item.title: VIDEO: I governed Osun for eight years without owning a house – Aregbesola
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former Osun State Governor Rauf Aregbesola claims he governed for eight years without owning a personal house. Watch the video of his assertion.
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-i-governed-osun-for-eight-years-without-owning-a-house-aregbesola/

item.title: El-Rufai’s family disowns viral letter to ADC leadership
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nasir El-Rufai’s family has disowned a viral letter purportedly from the former governor to ADC leadership, calling it fake. Read more on the El-Rufai fami
Read More: https://punchng.com/el-rufais-family-disowns-viral-letter-to-adc-leadership/

item.title: Arsenal complete deal to sign winger Tzolis
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Arsenal have announced the signing of winger Christos Tzolis from Club Brugge on a five-year deal.

item.title: England netball star Metcalf injured on eve of Commonwealth Games
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Kwara law officers threaten strike over unpaid entitlements
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kwara State law officers issue strike notice over unpaid allowances and harmonisation of salaries. Learn about their demands and the potential impact on ju
Read More: https://punchng.com/kwara-law-officers-threaten-strike-over-unpaid-entitlements/

item.title: NHRC records 600 human rights violation cases in Borno
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The NHRC in Borno has recorded over 600 human rights violation cases, including domestic violence and child abuse, between January and July 2026.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nhrc-records-600-human-rights-violation-cases-in-borno/

item.title: N319m fraud: A’Court denies ex-FCTA director bail, orders accelerated hearing
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Appeal Court has denied bail to Garba Dukku, former FCTA director, who was sentenced to 24 years for N319m fraud, ordering an accelerated hearing.
Read More: https://punchng.com/n319m-fraud-acourt-denies-ex-fcta-director-bail-orders-accelerated-hearing/

item.title: Police arrest four suspected kidnappers, rescue victim in Rivers
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Rivers State Police Command arrests four suspected kidnappers and rescues a victim abducted from her shop in Aluu community. Read more on the successful op
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-four-suspected-kidnappers-rescue-victim-in-rivers/

item.title: FIFA Reveals 20 Youngest Players To Ever Win World Cup
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Winning the FIFA World Cup is the pinnacle of international football, but only a few players have achieved the feat before turning 21. From Brazilian legend Pelé to Spain’s teenage sensation Lamine Yamal, these footballers etched their names into history by becoming world champions at remarkab

item.title: Sydney Talker opens up on celibacy journey
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Sydney also stated that he wishes to continue his celibacy journey for as long as possible.

item.title: Norway to make formal complaint to FIFA over Balogun red card saga
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Norway's football chief told The Times they are set to make a formal complaint to FIFA over the suspension of Folarin Balogun's World Cup ban because it has put "the whole game at risk". The post Norway to make formal complaint to FIFA over Balogun red card saga appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Liverpool must be difficult to beat - Iraola
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Manager Andoni Iraola says Liverpool fans "just want to see the team play well and deliver good results".

item.title: UK Withdraws Diplomatic Staff From Iran As Conflict Intensifies
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The United Kingdom has withdrawn its remaining diplomatic staff from Iran as the conflict between Iran and the United States continued to escalate, with a 12th consecutive night of airstrikes. In a statement published on its website, the UK Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) said, &

item.title: Timi Frank Counters Presidency, Says Trump’s Letter Didn’t Endorse Tinubu
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Former Deputy National Publicity Secretary of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Timi Frank, has said a letter from United States President Donald Trump to President Bola Tinubu should be regarded as a routine diplomatic communication rather than an endorsement of the Nigerian leader’s administrat

item.title: La Liga: Barcelona sign Adeyemi for $33m from Borussia Dortmund
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Barcelona strengthens attack, signing Karim Adeyemi from Borussia Dortmund for $33 million. The versatile forward joins Barca until 2031.
Read More: https://punchng.com/la-liga-barcelona-sign-adeyemi-for-33m-from-borussia-dortmund/

item.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.

item.title: France Approves Return of ‘Bee-Harming’ Pesticides
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: France’s parliament has approved a controversial bill that would allow the return of two pesticides previously banned in the country, prompting Environment Minister Monique Barbut to announce plans to resign. Barbut told her aides that she had “no other option” than to step down, saying she would su

item.title: Japan’s $1.8 trillion pension giant might bring money home. That could jolt U.S. stocks and the Fed.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Japan’s pension titan selling foreign assets could push U.S. yields up and sap demand for the dollar.

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Tech
item.description: Seychelles has officially opened its telecommunications market to Starlink, giving households and businesses access to satellite-based broadband and marking a

item.title: Hull City owner 'disturbed' by transfer claims
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Hull City owner Acun Ilicali says he is "disturbed" by claims of the club being rejected by transfer targets and, in an unusual move, attempted to clarify the situation.

item.title: Dev's Digest: Singh on set, Bhatia's Liverpool bid, Antonio's surprise return
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Welcome to Dev's Digest, a Sky Sports column in which Asian Football Award winner Dev Trehan delivers exclusive news and reflects on some of the key developments for British South Asians in Football.

item.title: Oriire: Stakeholders pledge stronger collaboration for peaceful coexistence in Oyo
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Stakeholders drawn from Fulani communities, security agencies, farmers’ groups, traditional institutions, and community leaders have reaffirmed their commitment to promoting peaceful coexistence and strengthening security across Oyo State. The commitment was made during a high-level stakeholde

item.title: JUST-IN: Illegal Miners Kill NSCDC Operative
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Some suspected illegal miners have shot dead an operative of the Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC) during an operation to clamp down on illegal mining activities in Korokpa Village, a suburb of Minna, the Niger State capital. The incident occurred while operatives of the NSCDC Mining

item.title: DSS To Appeal Judgment On Oriire Abduction Sentencing
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The DSS is not satisfied with the verdict, arguing that the sentencing is not commensurate with the crimes committed in Oriire. The post DSS To Appeal Judgment On Oriire Abduction Sentencing appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: US Stock Futures Drop as Traders Focus on AI Spending, Oil Jumps
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: US stock futures retreated as worries over artificial-intelligence spending weighed on sentiment while oil prices closed in on $100 a barrel. Traders braced for more key earnings from Intel Corp., due later in the afternoon.

The Minister of State for Foreign Affairs, Ambassador Sola Enikanolaiye, made

item.title: Anambra introduces plea bargain policy to decongest prisons, speed up justice

item.title: India's 'Cockroach' movement spreads to Mumbai after violence in New Delhi
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description:
Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has finally broken his silence on the student protest movement known as the “Cockroach Janta Party”.
The protests have spread from the capital New Delhi to other large cities including Mumbai and Bengaluru. Clashes with the police have occurred in several cities

item.title: Two Russian men jailed in Angola for terrorism and spying
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: The men were convicted over alleged plan to stage coup and organise deadly demonstrations over fuel prices last yearTwo Russian men have been found guilty by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying, after being accused of planning a coup and helping to organise deadly demonstrations in the southern

item.title: Cellnex Studies Strategic Options After Share Price Slump
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Cellnex Telecom SA is evaluating strategic options, according to people familiar with the matter, as the wireless tower operator looks at ways to grapple with its debt load and weak share price.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal High Court sitting in Abuja has sentenced three men to life imprisonment after finding them complicit in the abduction of students and teachers from a school in Orire Local Government Area of Oyo State. item.title: Russia warns US against Ukraine arms sales in Manila meeting
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov warned Washington against making "unacceptable" arms deliveries to Ukraine during a Thursday meeting in Manila with US counterpart Marco Rubio, Moscow said. The post Russia warns US against Ukraine arms sales in Manila meeting appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: EU Fines Google $1bn For Search Antitrust Violations
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The European Union has fined Google 890 million euros, equivalent to approximately $1bn, after finding that the technology giant violated digital antitrust rules by using its Google Play app store and search engine to steer consumers toward its own services and applications at the expense of competi

item.title: GCM Grosvenor Raises $1.2 Billion for Credit Secondaries Push
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: GCM Grosvenor has raised $1.2 billion for its first dedicated fund strategy targeting credit secondaries.

item.title: Africa: Africa's Food Insecurity Eases for First Time in a Decade. The Next Task is Making the Gains Harder to Reverse
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [allAfrica] Across Africa last year, fewer people were uncertain about their ability to obtain adequate food or were forced to reduce the quality and quantity of what they ate. It was the first continent-wide improvement in food insecurity in a decade. The Hunger rate declined for the first time in

item.description: Nigeria emerged as Airtel Africa Plc’s strongest growth market in the quarter ended June 30, 2026, with the telecom operator posting a 50.4 per cent increase in reported revenue, supported by strong demand for data services, higher customer spending and the full impact of tariff adjustments im

Fashola, who made this declaration while speaking on TVC, said he av

item.title: China’s zero-tariff window offers fresh hope for Nigerian businesses
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to the ambassador, the Nigerian mission in China has already begun consultations to understand how the policy will operate in practice and to ensure businesses and other stakeholders are adequately informed.

item.title: US Secret Service investigates agent over alleged disclosure of Vance travel details
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The agent has not been publicly identified, and no criminal charges have been filed. Administrative leave is a standard measure that allows an employee to remain off duty while an investigation is carried out.

item.description: Nigeria does not need to discover a new source of external capital. One of its largest and most resilient sources already exists.

item.title: UK withdraws diplomatic staff from Iran as conflict deepens

item.description: The UK government also renewed its advice against all travel to Iran, warning British nationals that the security situation remains highly volatile and could deteriorate further with little or no notice.

The versatile 24-year-old is

At the company’s annual gener

item.title: Done Deal: Durosinmi Moves To Belgian Club KRC Genk
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Rafiu Durosinmi has sealed a permanent transfer to Belgian Pro League outfit Rafiu Durosinmi, Completesports.com reports. Durosinmi, who joins the Smurfs from Italian outfit Pisa penned a four-year contract. The Nigeria international is Genk’s second signing of the summer. The striker previous

item.title: Leeds in talks with Man City over deal for Trafford
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Leeds United are in talks with Manchester City over a deal for goalkeeper James Trafford.

item.title: Alphabet’s Century Bond Has Now Lost a 10th of Its Face Value
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A 100-year bond that Alphabet Inc. issued earlier this year in the sterling market as it gorged on debt around the world has dropped below 90 pence on the pound for the first time.

item.title: Barcelona complete signing of Karim Adeyemi from Dortmund
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Barcelona have completed the signing of Karim Adeyemi from Borussia Dortmund, with the German forward joining the Spanish champions on a five-year contract in a deal worth up to €29 million. The post Barcelona complete signing of Karim Adeyemi from Dortmund appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Transcorp Group reports N75.9 billion pre-tax profit in H1 2026
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Transnational Corporation Plc (Transcorp) has released its H1 2026 results for the period ended June 30, 2026, reporting a pretax profit of N75.9 billion, representing an 11.45% year-on-year decline from N85.7 billion in H1 2025. The post Transcorp Group reports N75.9 billion pre-tax profit in H1 20

item.title: Customs warns Nigerians against fake recruitment update
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: “Members of the public are advised to disregard the publication and rely only on information disseminated through the official communication channels of the NCS. The post Customs warns Nigerians against fake recruitment update appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Pandur joined Rangers after Hull planned new number one
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: New Rangers goalkeeper Ivor Pandur says he passed up the chance to be part of Hull City's return to the Premier League after being told they planned to sign a new number one this summer.
